content.pattern: Working on copilot-core using copilot-core itself (dogfooding) requires extra care because changes propagate instantly to all downstream projects via symlinks.
content.when_to_use: Every session where Leo is working inside the copilot-core repo itself (self_hosting: true in project-config.yml)
how: Propagation is instant: editing rules/, agents/, skills/ here affects all projects on the next session. No staging. Escalation-triggers rule applies — Leo never edits a rule without R2
Changes in a Manager propagate via inheritance: before changing a core Manager, ask 'does any project that extends this depend on current behavior?' If yes, include migration note in commit
Editing Leo mid-session: the current session keeps old version in memory. Open new session to validate behavior changes
No other projects to escalate to: meta-work is not a license for direct mode. Think-before-execute and escalation-triggers still apply without exception
Core artifacts locked to English: enforced via project-config.yml locales.project_files: en
Recommended flow: owner describes intent → Leo applies think-before-execute → structural decision = alignment mode → proposal for R2 → approved → edit → evidence → wrap-up on owner signal
